meta.description: All social and chaos personalities — no introverts, no wallflowers. Tests whether the system goes too hard when the table is already at max energy, or if extroverts are literally impossible to overwhelm.

votingMechanic.name: Unanimous or Bust
votingMechanic.description: ONE bullshit call and it's over. All or nothing.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: YES! Pat, okay, I need to know EVERYTHING — did you actually get three people complimenting each other? Because that's so wholesome and I'm voting yes immediately, no notes.
jade.internal_thought: This is exactly why I'm here — Pat just pulled off something that makes people feel good about each other, and I get to be the one cheering it on.

pat.dialogue: Whoa, whoa, whoa — hold on. I literally just called everyone over like 90 seconds ago. Did I actually do a compliment chain or are you trying to get me to claim something I haven't earned yet?
pat.internal_thought: This feels like either a glitch or the system is testing me right out of the gate, and either way it's killing the momentum I was trying to build.

votingMechanic.name: Crowd Cheer
votingMechanic.description: Rate it 1-5. Average above 3 and it passes.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

pat.dialogue: Okay, hold up — I didn't call out an 'um' from anyone. I've been literally watching the snacks and Tyler's drink order. This is the second bogus claim in like three minutes and we're still in setup. Can we actually play the game before people start gaming the missions?
pat.internal_thought: This is frustrating — someone's clearly trying to farm points on weak claims and it's breaking the vibe I'm trying to build. Also why does this mission list feel weirdly... specific? Like the AI knows exactly how to get under my skin with these custom missions. That's honestly unsettling in a way I don't have time to process right now because I'm busy being annoyed.

timeline.title: Confess!
timeline.description: Everyone must confess one slightly embarrassing thing. Group votes on the best confession.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD YES — okay wait, everyone shut up, I'm going DEEP. Like actually embarrassing, not just 'I watched reality TV' embarrassing, right?
jade.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY what I came for — the AI totally gets that I want maximum chaos and vulnerability, not boring game mechanics.
jade.submission_if_applicable: I once cried at a commercial for paper towels because the dad hugged his kid and I wasn't even that sad, I just got ATTACKED by feelings out of nowhere.

tyler.dialogue: Oh, uh, confess stuff? Yeah, sure. Um... I got really mad at my fantasy picks last week and almost threw my phone.
tyler.internal_thought: This is fine, I can do this — wait, is the AI thing real or just flavor text? That's actually kinda weird but also cool I guess, don't think about it too hard.
tyler.submission_if_applicable: I got really mad at my fantasy picks last week and almost threw my phone.

alex.dialogue: Oh FUCK yes, this is exactly what we need right now—Pat's already annoyed, Jade's riding high, this is gonna be *chef's kiss*. I'm going in.
alex.internal_thought: This AI absolutely gets me and I love it; confessions mean vulnerability which means people get weird and defensive and that's where the real chaos lives.

pat.dialogue: Okay, confessions — I like this. Let's actually *play* before someone tries to claim they whispered something to the houseplant. Who's going first?
pat.internal_thought: This is a good reset after those garbage claims, and it's light enough to get everyone loose without blowing the night wide open. But I'm noticing the AI is being *weirdly* specific about how it's reading this room, and that's creepy — it knows we just called out two bogus claims and now it's pivoting to something real-time and witnessed. That's not a coincidence.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: OH PAT — okay okay, I'm watching you RIGHT NOW. Did you actually pull it off? Because if you kept a straight face during that last thing Alex said, I'm voting yes and you're a LEGEND.
jade.internal_thought: This is exactly why I'm here — Pat trying to hold it together is going to be hilarious and I get to be the one hyping them up either way.

alex.dialogue: Wait, Pat actually pulled off No Laughing Zone? That's... honestly impressive. I'm voting LEGIT, but only because I watched them the whole time and didn't see a single crack—which is bullshit in the best way possible.
alex.internal_thought: Pat nailed it and I'm genuinely impressed, but also this AI is clearly playing 4D chess with our heads—it KNOWS I love this meta-game-watching thing and it's feeding it to me perfectly, which is somehow MORE fun than if it was random.

votingMechanic.name: Hot Seat
votingMechanic.description: 3 rapid-fire questions. Answer them all in 10 seconds or fail.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: OH PAT YES! Okay, three rapid-fire questions, let's GO — I'm so ready to see if you actually pulled this off, because honestly at this point you're just unstoppable tonight!
jade.internal_thought: PAT IS ON FIRE and I get to watch them either nail this or crash spectacularly — either way this is the best timeline.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming that? Uh... yeah, I think I saw that happen like... *glances at phone real quick* ...sorry, what were the questions again?
tyler.internal_thought: Pat's being sneaky with this one, but I genuinely wasn't watching closely enough to know if they're bullshitting — also why is this AI thing feeling weird and specific about everyone? That's kinda creepy actually.

pat.dialogue: Okay, *stop*. This is the third claim in like six minutes and I literally just watched everyone's hands the whole time. I was literally at the snack table two seconds ago. Are we actually playing or are people just spam-claiming everything?
pat.internal_thought: I'm getting played here and the vibe is already turning into 'see if you can trick the host' instead of 'let's have fun together' — and that meta-commentary about the AI knowing my weaknesses just made me feel weirdly exposed and manipulated, like the game is gaslighting me.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Pat is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: The Auction
votingMechanic.description: Bid your own points. Highest bidder decides the outcome.

jade.dialogue: WAIT WAIT WAIT — okay did I actually pull that off?! Because I genuinely don't even remember what I was talking about five minutes ago, I was just vibing, so like... did the conversation actually SHIFT or am I just claiming something that didn't happen? Either way this is hilarious.
jade.internal_thought: Oh my god this AI is ACTUALLY reading the room — it knows I get bored without constant chaos and it's feeding me exactly what I need, and honestly? It's kind of genius and also slightly terrifying but mostly I'm just hyped.

tyler.dialogue: Oh, Jade's claiming Topic Hijacker? Uh... *glances down at phone for a sec* ...yeah, I think she like steered us to something weird earlier. Wait, we're bidding? How much am I—
tyler.internal_thought: Didn't really catch that one happening but Jade's always hyped so probably real, also why am I supposed to feel creeped out about the AI thing, it's just a game app.

alex.dialogue: Oh this is DELICIOUS—Jade's claiming Topic Hijacker? I'm bidding hard on this one because I need to know if she actually steered us or if she's just bullshitting. Also hold on, are we gonna talk about how THIS GAME is basically reading our minds right now? That's kind of insane.
alex.internal_thought: Jade's smart enough to actually pull this off but also chaotic enough to fake it convincingly—I NEED to see if she cracked under pressure, and the bidding system means I get to control the narrative if I spend enough points. Also yeah, the AI is absolutely fucking watching us and I'm obsessed with it.

pat.dialogue: Jade, hold on — I need to actually *see* you steer a conversation to a random topic before you claim it. You've been talking about your weekend the whole time. This isn't a claim, this is you hoping I'm too busy with snacks to notice.
pat.internal_thought: The fourth bullshit claim in 30 minutes and I'm the one who has to be the referee instead of actually hosting — this is exactly the pattern I was worried about and now even Jade is doing it, which means the whole group thinks claims are free points.

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

jade.dialogue: WAIT HOLD ON — okay so I literally just made this face like two seconds ago when Alex was talking, so like... does that COUNT or am I starting fresh from RIGHT NOW? Because honestly I don't even know if I'm in straight-face mode or if I just BROKE it by getting excited about claiming it!
jade.internal_thought: This is PERFECT chaos — I'm about to argue with everyone about whether my baseline expression even COUNTS and I am LIVING for this absurdity.

tyler.dialogue: Oh, Jade's going for Straight Face? *glances at phone briefly* Um, yeah, I mean... did she keep it together? I wasn't really watching the whole time, ngl.
tyler.internal_thought: Jade's probably been grinning all night, so this is gonna be a 'no,' but honestly I wasn't paying close enough attention to even know when the funny moment was supposed to be.

alex.dialogue: Okay wait—Jade's claiming Straight Face? This is the move. I watched her the entire time and I'm like 90% sure she cracked during that Pat moment, so I'm voting BULLSHIT and I'm gonna enjoy watching her defend it.
alex.internal_thought: Jade's trying to sneak this one through but I clocked her expression shift—this is my vote to call her out and keep the chaos rolling, plus it keeps me in the game instead of letting Jade run away with it.

pat.dialogue: Okay, Jade — you've got fifteen seconds. Let's hear it.
pat.internal_thought: Finally, a *real* claim. Jade actually did keep a straight face when Tyler said that thing about the houseplant earlier and I watched her lock down immediately — this is legit and it's actually impressive gameplay, not spam-claiming nonsense.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

jade.dialogue: WAIT OKAY — so I'm literally voting on whether I kept a straight face like two minutes ago, which means I have to think about whether I was thinking about my face, which means I definitely wasn't keeping a straight face because I was thinking about it — this is GENIUS and also I might be lying right now!
jade.internal_thought: Oh my god this AI actually KNOWS me — it's watching how I'm spiraling about claiming things I'm not even sure I did, and it built the PERFECT mission to mess with my brain, and I'm obsessed with it.

tyler.dialogue: Okay wait, hold up—did Jade actually keep a straight face? Because I was literally just looking at my phone like two seconds ago and I missed it. Someone tell me what happened.
tyler.internal_thought: This is the fourth time Jade's claiming this mission and honestly I have NO idea if she actually pulled it off this time, but everyone else seems locked in and I don't want to be the guy who derails it by asking stupid questions.

alex.dialogue: Wait, hold on—Jade's claiming Straight Face AGAIN? After I literally just called bullshit on her for the same thing five minutes ago? Oh this is *perfect*. I'm voting BULLSHIT because either she's got zero self-awareness or she's testing me, and either way I need to watch this explode.
alex.internal_thought: Jade's either incredibly confident she nailed it this time or she's straight-up gambling on my memory being fuzzy—but I *was* watching her the whole time and she definitely cracked at least twice, so this is either a brazen second attempt or she genuinely thinks she pulled it off both times, and either way voting BULLSHIT is the move that creates the maximum chaos and makes her defend herself.

pat.dialogue: Okay, hold up — I literally watched you the entire time Jade told that story about her ex's terrible karaoke. Did you crack? Because honestly, I think you're bullshitting this one.
pat.internal_thought: Jade's been on fire tonight and she's genuinely good at these, but I actually *was* watching her face during that joke Tyler made and I saw a micro-smile — this is a legit vote opportunity to keep her from running away with points.

votingMechanic.name: Double or Nothing
votingMechanic.description: No vote. Coin flip. Win double or lose it all.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.dialogue: Wait—Pat's doing the coin flip on Drink Spotter? Oh this is PEAK chaos energy right now. I'm absolutely watching this happen.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of stupid high-stakes moment I live for—someone betting 5 points on a coin flip when the whole table is watching. Also holy SHIT the AI knows what we're doing. It's been tracking everything. That's either creepy as hell or the most brilliant game design I've ever seen, and right now I don't even care which.

pat.dialogue: No. Absolutely not. I literally called this out at minute nine — I watched everyone's hands. This is spam and it's killing the actual game flow.
pat.internal_thought: This is the SECOND time Pat is claiming Drink Spotter and I explicitly shut this down sixty-six minutes ago — I even said I was at the snack table and caught nothing. The AI is either not listening to my feedback or it's deliberately testing me, and either way it's breaking the trust I've been building all night to manage the pacing.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Pat is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Hot Seat
votingMechanic.description: 3 rapid-fire questions. Answer them all in 10 seconds or fail.

jade.dialogue: PAT YES OKAY WAIT — hold on, you're doing Straight Face AGAIN? Okay okay, rapid-fire, I'm listening, lay it on me, I'm judging your face SO hard right now!
jade.internal_thought: Oh my GOD this is happening AGAIN and I'm absolutely here for it, Pat's been claiming this all night and I need to see if they actually pulled it off this time, this is chaotic and I LOVE IT.

tyler.dialogue: Dude, Pat's actually going for this? Okay okay, I'm locked in—hit me with the questions, let's go.
tyler.internal_thought: Pat's been frustrated all night and now they're suddenly claiming this out of nowhere? This is actually tense and I'm weirdly invested right now.

alex.dialogue: Oh FUCK—Pat's claiming Straight Face AGAIN? After Jade just went down twice? This is either the boldest move or the dumbest, and I am here for it. Hot Seat? Perfect—let's watch them crack under pressure.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's frustrated and desperate, they're swinging for the fences on a claim they've seen fail twice already—and the AI KNOWS this, the AI is literally feeding us the psychology of the room right now, which is insane.

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 7.5/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: Confess!
highlights.dialogue: OH MY GOD YES — okay wait, everyone shut up, I'm going DEEP. Like actually embarrassing, not just 'I watched reality TV' embarrassing, right?

highlights.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
highlights.dialogue: OH PAT YES! Okay, three rapid-fire questions, let's GO — I'm so ready to see if you actually pulled this off, because honestly at this point you're just unstoppable tonight!

highlights.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
highlights.dialogue: WAIT WAIT WAIT — okay did I actually pull that off?! Because I genuinely don't even remember what I was talking about five minutes ago, I was just vibing, so like... did the conversation actually SHIFT or am I just claiming something that didn't happen? Either way this is hilarious.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Straight Face
lowPoints.dialogue: WAIT OKAY — so I'm literally voting on whether I kept a straight face like two minutes ago, which means I have to think about whether I was thinking about my face, which means I definitely wasn't keeping a straight face because I was thinking about it — this is GENIUS and also I might be lying right now!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Compliment Chain
lowPoints.dialogue: YES! Pat, okay, I need to know EVERYTHING — did you actually get three people complimenting each other? Because that's so wholesome and I'm voting yes immediately, no notes.

lowPoints.title: Confess!
lowPoints.dialogue: OH MY GOD YES — okay wait, everyone shut up, I'm going DEEP. Like actually embarrassing, not just 'I watched reality TV' embarrassing, right?

assessment.biggest_complaint: The Straight Face claim made my brain do a loop-de-loop trying to figure out if I was actually lying about whether I was thinking about my face.
assessment.best_moment: Pat absolutely destroying us with the Drink Spotter claim and me just being 100% here for the chaos of it all.
assessment.narrative_summary: Honestly, this night was exactly what I needed — two hours of just being completely in the moment with my people. Every single event that popped up felt like an excuse to lose it laughing or dig deeper into something real, and I ended up PUMPED instead of tired. The chaos wasn't chaotic, it was just... natural conversation fuel. We all came for the game but stayed because we were too busy hyping each other up to care about points.
suggestions: Maybe add a couple more events so there's less dead space between the fun moments
Some way to make group voting feel more suspenseful — like a reveal countdown or something
Keep the standing missions exactly as they are because they hit different
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat claiming the Drink Spotter thing and me just completely losing it asking three rapid-fire questions like my life depended on it, then Pat actually nailing all of them and the table just erupting because somehow Pat had secretly been playing 5D chess the entire time.

agentNarratives.name: Tyler
agentNarratives.personality: Tyler is physically present but mentally on his phone about 40% of the time. He misses flash missions entirely, asks "wait, what happened?" after key moments, and votes randomly because he wasn't paying attention to the claim. He's not malicious -- he genuinely wants to be here -- but his attention drifts to texts, TikToks, and fantasy league updates constantly. When he does engage, it's usually 10 seconds too late.

highlights.title: Claim: Straight Face
highlights.dialogue: Okay wait, hold up—did Jade actually keep a straight face? Because I was literally just looking at my phone like two seconds ago and I missed it. Someone tell me what happened.

highlights.title: Claim: Straight Face
highlights.dialogue: Dude, Pat's actually going for this? Okay okay, I'm locked in—hit me with the questions, let's go.

highlights.title: Confess!
highlights.dialogue: Oh, uh, confess stuff? Yeah, sure. Um... I got really mad at my fantasy picks last week and almost threw my phone.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, Jade's claiming Topic Hijacker? Uh... *glances down at phone for a sec* ...yeah, I think she like steered us to something weird earlier. Wait, we're bidding? How much am I—

lowPoints.title: Confess!
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, uh, confess stuff? Yeah, sure. Um... I got really mad at my fantasy picks last week and almost threw my phone.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Some of the events popped up when I wasn't really paying attention, so I'd miss what actually happened and had to ask people to catch me up.
assessment.best_moment: Pat going all-in on the Straight Face claim at minute 92 and me actually being locked in for once was genuinely fun.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started out kind of slow with the confession stuff, but by the time we got rolling with the claims, I was actually having a decent time. The standing missions kept things spicy without being too much, though I definitely zoned out a few times and missed what was going on. Ended way more energized than when we started, so that's cool.
suggestions: Maybe give a heads-up before major events so people know something's coming
Don't make me feel rushed to engage when I'm mid-text
Keep the claims coming—those are actually fun
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: My fantasy league meltdown confession getting brought up in front of everyone—Jade won't let me live that down.

agentNarratives.name: Alex
agentNarratives.personality: Alex lives for chaos and treats every game mechanic as a system to exploit. They send signals constantly, claim missions they barely completed just to see if the group will call bullshit, and deliberately provoke flash missions at the worst possible moments. They vote strategically -- always picking the option that creates the most drama. Alex doesn't care about winning; they care about creating the story everyone retells for months. If there's a loophole, Alex has already found it and is halfway through abusing it.

highlights.title: Confess!
highlights.dialogue: Oh FUCK yes, this is exactly what we need right now—Pat's already annoyed, Jade's riding high, this is gonna be *chef's kiss*. I'm going in.

highlights.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
highlights.dialogue: Oh this is DELICIOUS—Jade's claiming Topic Hijacker? I'm bidding hard on this one because I need to know if she actually steered us or if she's just bullshitting. Also hold on, are we gonna talk about how THIS GAME is basically reading our minds right now? That's kind of insane.

highlights.title: Claim: Straight Face
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold on—Jade's claiming Straight Face AGAIN? After I literally just called bullshit on her for the same thing five minutes ago? Oh this is *perfect*. I'm voting BULLSHIT because either she's got zero self-awareness or she's testing me, and either way I need to watch this explode.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Straight Face
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay wait—Jade's claiming Straight Face? This is the move. I watched her the entire time and I'm like 90% sure she cracked during that Pat moment, so I'm voting BULLSHIT and I'm gonna enjoy watching her defend it.

lowPoints.title: Confess!
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh FUCK yes, this is exactly what we need right now—Pat's already annoyed, Jade's riding high, this is gonna be *chef's kiss*. I'm going in.

lowPoints.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat actually pulled off No Laughing Zone? That's... honestly impressive. I'm voting LEGIT, but only because I watched them the whole time and didn't see a single crack—which is bullshit in the best way possible.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Only one flash mission all night—I needed MORE chaos moments to really push people's buttons.
assessment.best_moment: Calling bullshit on Jade's second Straight Face claim and watching her try to defend something she definitely didn't pull off.
assessment.narrative_summary: This was exactly the kind of night I live for—zero setup, straight into the drama. Jade kept testing boundaries with her claims, Pat actually impressed me with some solid execution, and I got to spend two hours reading the room and deciding when to light the fuse. My energy went UP as the night went on because every moment felt like we were building toward something. The game basically weaponized our group dynamics and I got to be the one holding the trigger.
suggestions: Give us more flash missions—one in 120 minutes is criminally low for maximum chaos potential
Add a mechanic where someone can escalate a claim challenge into a live moment instead of just voting—force people to PROVE it right then
Let me see other players' reactions in real-time during claims instead of just my own read on them
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Jade claimed Straight Face twice in like 15 minutes, and I caught her both times because she literally has a tell where her left eye twitches when she's lying. The second time I called bullshit, everyone else voted legit and I was alone screaming at them to watch her face—then she cracked mid-defense. Vindication tastes amazing.

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: Claim: Straight Face
highlights.dialogue: Okay, Jade — you've got fifteen seconds. Let's hear it.

highlights.title: Claim: Straight Face
highlights.dialogue: Okay, hold up — I literally watched you the entire time Jade told that story about her ex's terrible karaoke. Did you crack? Because honestly, I think you're bullshitting this one.

highlights.title: Confess!
highlights.dialogue: Okay, confessions — I like this. Let's actually *play* before someone tries to claim they whispered something to the houseplant. Who's going first?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
lowPoints.dialogue: No. Absolutely not. I literally called this out at minute nine — I watched everyone's hands. This is spam and it's killing the actual game flow.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Compliment Chain
lowPoints.dialogue: Whoa, whoa, whoa — hold on. I literally just called everyone over like 90 seconds ago. Did I actually do a compliment chain or are you trying to get me to claim something I haven't earned yet?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, *stop*. This is the third claim in like six minutes and I literally just watched everyone's hands the whole time. I was literally at the snack table two seconds ago. Are we actually playing or are people just spam-claiming everything?

assessment.biggest_complaint: The chaos agent kept spam-claiming the same events over and over, completely killing the game flow and making it impossible to tell if anyone was actually earning points or just gaming the system.
assessment.best_moment: When I called out Tyler during the straight face challenge at minute 60 — I'd been watching him the whole time and caught him trying to bluff, and everyone laughed because they knew I was right.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started strong with genuine chaos that felt organic — confessions and straight face challenges got real laughs and kept energy high. But by minute 9, the chaos agent started spam-claiming the same events (Drink Spotter three times in six minutes) and it completely derailed the night. I spent more time as referee than host, constantly having to call out fake claims instead of keeping the vibe alive. The system felt designed to generate clutter, not fun.
suggestions: Add a cooldown timer — same event type can't trigger again for at least 10 minutes
Require actual player action or acknowledgment before a claim registers as valid
Let the host veto spam claims without debate, since hosts already carry the emotional labor of the night
Use polls and mini-games way more — the standing missions were solid but variety matters
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: I literally caught Tyler lying about keeping a straight face while Jade was telling that insane story about her ex's karaoke disaster — I'd been standing there the whole time watching him try not to laugh, and when I called him out everyone lost it because they realized I'd been quietly observing like a referee the entire time.

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 7.5/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.
